set -e
DIR="/tmp/sv-7a34a4864d854627a61ebf3f607fba1e"
SRC="$DIR/AccessControl/AccessChecker.cs"
BAK="$SRC.bak"
RESULTS=""
run_test() {

    cd "$DIR"
    result=$(dotnet test AccessControl.Tests/AccessControl.Tests.csproj --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -3)
    if echo "$result" | grep -q "Passed!"; then
        RESULTS="$RESULTS\n[SURVIVED] $name  <-- UNDETECTED BUG"
    else
        RESULTS="$RESULTS\n[KILLED]   $name"
    fi
    cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
}

# Build once to restore
cd "$DIR" && dotnet build AccessControl.Tests/AccessControl.Tests.csproj --quiet 2>/dev/null

# Mutation 1: Editor on system resource gets CanWrite=true (should be false)
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.Editor => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' "$SRC"
run_test "M1: Editor+system => CanWrite=true (privilege escalation)"

# Mutation 2: Guest gets CanRead=true (removing denial)
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/_ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = false, CanWrite = false }/_ => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/' "$SRC"
run_test "M2: Guest gets CanRead=true (broken access denial)"

# Mutation 3: User on system resource gets CanRead=true (remove system restriction)
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
# The default branch handles User+system. Change it to grant read.
# Actually User+system falls to default. Let's add a line before default.
# Simpler: change default to give read access -- that's M2. 
# Instead let's change the User branch to not check isSystem
sed -i 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.User => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/' "$SRC"
run_test "M3: User reads system resources (bypasses system check)"

# Mutation 4: User gets CanWrite=true on normal resources
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = false }/Role.User when !isSystem => new Permission { Resource = resource, CanRead = true, CanWrite = true }/' "$SRC"
run_test "M4: User gets CanWrite on normal resources (privilege escalation)"

# Mutation 5: CanPerform flips the ternary (writeAccess check returns CanRead instead of CanWrite)
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/return writeAccess ? perm.CanWrite : perm.CanRead;/return writeAccess ? perm.CanRead : perm.CanWrite;/' "$SRC"
run_test "M5: CanPerform ternary flipped (write checks read permission)"

# Mutation 6: Remove null/empty token guard in ElevateRole
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i '/if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(token))/,/return currentRole;/{/return currentRole;/d;/if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(token))/d}' "$SRC"
run_test "M6: ElevateRole null/empty token guard removed"

# Mutation 7: Guest can elevate to Editor with ELEVATE-EDITOR token
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/if (token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" && currentRole >= Role.User)/if (token == "ELEVATE-EDITOR" \&\& currentRole >= Role.Guest)/' "$SRC"
run_test "M7: Guest can elevate to Editor (broken role check)"

# Mutation 8: User can elevate to Admin with ELEVATE-ADMIN
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/if (token == "ELEVATE-ADMIN" && currentRole >= Role.Editor)/if (token == "ELEVATE-ADMIN" \&\& currentRole >= Role.User)/' "$SRC"
run_test "M8: User can elevate to Admin (broken elevation guard)"

# Mutation 9: Case-sensitive system resource check (removing OrdinalIgnoreCase)
cp "$BAK" "$SRC"
sed -i 's/resource.StartsWith("sys\/", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)/resource.StartsWith("sys\/")/' "$SRC"
run_test "M9: System check now case-sensitive (SYS/ bypasses)"

echo ""
echo "========== MUTATION TESTING RESULTS =========="
echo -e "$RESULTS"
echo ""
echo "SURVIVED = tests did NOT catch the bug (coverage gap)"
echo "KILLED   = tests correctly caught the bug"
